🔥 Fresh off the fire, nightly

Big, happy, fire food.

Sorrel is your seasonal neighborhood spot in Hayes Valley — market veg, a roaring hearth, hand-rolled pasta, and natural wine worth the trip.

Our story

From a market stall to your favorite table.

We cook almost everything over live oak and almond wood — that flavor can't be faked with a dial. Bread is milled and baked in-house, and veg arrives twice a week from growers we know by name.

Whole-animal, low-waste, zero theatre. Just really good food and people who want to feed you.

The menu · this week

Today's spread

Written each morning around the market. A taste of tonight.

★ House favorite

Whole fish on the coals

Line-caught, grilled over oak till the skin crackles. Salsa verde, lemon, sea salt.

Pasta

Hand-cut tagliatelle

Slow ragù of the day, brown butter, garden herbs.

Snacks

Hearth bread & butter

Stone-milled levain, smoked salt.

From the fire

Dry-aged beef, shared

Local ranch, embers, bone marrow, watercress.

Garden

Charred little gems

Anchovy, sheep's cheese, breadcrumb.

Sweets

Olive-oil cake

Roasted stone fruit, crème fraîche, honey.

Book the hearth menu
Why Sorrel

Four happy reasons

Live-fire kitchen

Oak & almond wood, no gas line, no shortcuts.

Market-led

The menu changes daily with the harvest.

Natural wine

A short, low-intervention list — ask and we'll pour.

Walk-ins welcome

Pull up a stool at the bar. No dress code, ever.

How a night works

Easy as 1-2-3

1

Reserve

Book a table or counter seat online. Bar kept open for walk-ins.

2

Come hungry

Settle in with a glass and tell us about any allergies.

3

Dig in

Order à la carte, or let the kitchen send out the best of today.

The reel

Inside Sorrel

The hearth Tonight's pasta The room Natural pours Morning box House levain
Good to know

Quick questions

Do you take walk-ins?+
Yes — the bar and a handful of counter seats are kept for walk-ins every night. The dining room books up, so reserve ahead for a table at a set time.
Allergies & dietary needs?+
Always. Tell us when you book or sit down. Because we cook to the market we can flex most dishes, with strong vegetable and gluten-free options nightly.
Is there a tasting menu?+
Yes — the Hearth Menu is a chef's selection of the day's best, served family-style. Book the counter to watch it come together.
Do you do corkage?+
We do, for a per-bottle fee, on bottles not on our list. Share a taste with the team and it's on us.
Where should I park?+
Street parking around Hayes Valley plus the Civic Center garage two blocks east. Short walk from Van Ness Muni; a bike rack out front.

Private dining & events

We'll cook across the city

Buy out the room or bring the hearth to you — dinners & events around the Bay:

Hayes ValleyNoPaThe MissionPacific HeightsMission BayOakland

Credentials

All above board

  • SF DPH health rating: A
  • Certified SF Green Business
  • Fully licensed & insured bar
  • Good Food Purchasing member
